jobDescription:
The Details:

Permanent Full Time | 9 day fortnight
From $99,136 pa up to $106,975 pa+ 12% super (level 5-6)
72.5 hour fortnight
Hybrid, flexible working environment, including some work from home days.
Location: Strathpine
Join City of Moreton Bay as our Digital Producer, and help inform and engage our diverse communities as we continue to grow and evolve as a City!

Our Corporate Communications team are on the search for a creative mind to work collaboratively across our organisation, planning and producing video and graphic content for communication channels including social media, website, and external media.

We will look to you to provide digital media production and communications advice across a wide range of council key stakeholders.

You'll also:

Design and create multimedia and digital content including video, audio, animation and photography.
Collaborate with key stakeholders across Council to design communication tools that will help Council to share project milestones and engage with the community.
Create and edit videos, photographs for use in print, on social media, digital platforms and Council’s website, with the potential for broadcast distribution.
Create engaging graphics and animation for use in videos to communicate Council messaging in an attractive way; using these graphics to simplify complex and detailed issues that Council wishes to communicate to the broader community.
Provide solutions and advice to achieve positive media and communications outcomes in relation to messaging and videos to support the Mayor, Councillors and other key stakeholders across Council.
Develop concepts with departments to help them conceive graphics, videos and animations to creatively illustrate their programs and initiatives.
Manage and maintain technical equipment, including cameras, lenses, lighting and audio equipment.
 Position Description - Digital Producer.pdf

About you

We are seeking someone who has a creative, strategic and planned approach to their work. You will be a collaborator with the ability to work with a wide variety of people - giving direction, providing insight and getting the job done!

You will have;

experience producing video and graphic content for communication channels including social media, website, and external media
excellence in Adobe creative suite specifically video editing and animation software - Premiere and After Effects
proficiency in the creation of graphics for use in animation and branding specifically Adobe Illustrator, Photoshop and Blender 3D software
experience directing subjects in a video in a polite but effective manner to ensure desired outcomes are achieved
experience operating camera drones, or be a fast learner and willing to give it a go. 
Why City of Moreton Bay?

What you do with your life matters. It’s the same with your career.

We’re the third-largest council in Australia and one of the fastest-growing areas. As Australia’s newest city, we’re building something that’s never been done before: a new kind of city, inspired and empowered by a new kind of council.

This is your opportunity to develop skills and experience in a truly unique place, at a truly unique point in time.

At City of Moreton Bay, we’re focused on the vital work we do today. But we’re also focused on the future. On what can be – for our city, our Council and your career.

Here’s what you’ll love about a career with us:

See it. To build a city of the future, first, you have to see it. The polycentric city we’re building will bring greater prosperity, sustainability and affordability to our city. This is your chance to contribute to something that’s never been done before.
Shape it. You’ll get hands-on in a rewarding career, serving and strengthening our communities for today and tomorrow. Our work is diverse, interesting and it matters – and it’s yours to shape and own.
Live it. Contribute to the city you and your family want to live in and enjoy the lifestyle you deserve. And if you don’t live here, you’ll still find pride and connection in so many ways with our successful, resilient team.
You’ll also enjoy a range of other benefits:

Find a better work-life balance with a nine-day (72.5-hour) fortnight, plus a hybrid, flexible working environment with some work-from-home days.
Negotiated salary increases locked in through our EBA - 4% in July 2026 and a further 4% in July 2027 (or CPI capped at 4.5%, whichever is greater).
Advance your skills and career through our Study, Training And Research Scheme (STARS).
Make your pay go further with salary packaging options on novated leases, superannuation payments, smartphones and devices.
Prioritise your health and wellbeing with discounted health insurance, free annual flu vaccinations and our Fitness Passport Program.
Get support when you and your family need it most with our 24/7 confidential counselling service, and Mates in Construction program.
Our Strathpine office has local parking on site and is close to public transport networks, making for an easy commute.

data.persona: A senior digital producer with 23 years of experience, blending government-grade reliability, full-spectrum production, and creative capability building.
data.targetJobTitle: Digital Producer at City of Moreton Bay
data.professionalProfile: Senior Digital Media Producer with 23 years of experience in government-grade creative production, full-spectrum multimedia content creation, and team mentoring. Proven track record in delivering WCAG-compliant, high-impact video and graphic content for social media, websites, and external media. Expert in Adobe Creative Suite (Premiere Pro, After Effects, Photoshop, Illustrator) and drone operations. Adept at translating complex council initiatives into engaging visual communications.
data.coverLetter: As a senior digital producer with 23 years of experience, I bring a strategic, full-spectrum approach to content creation that directly addresses City of Moreton Bay's need for engaging, compliant multimedia communications. I have over 10 years of experience producing video and graphic content for communication channels including social media, website, and external media—for example, leading video production at Crewcible and designing accessible social assets for government clients at Dionysus. I am proficient in Adobe Creative Suite with specific excellence in Premiere Pro and After Effects for video editing and animation, and I regularly use Photoshop and Illustrator for graphic creation. While I have not used Blender 3D, I am a fast learner and eager to master it. I have extensive experience directing subjects in video shoots, ensuring clear communication and desired outcomes. I am an Australian Permanent Resident with a 4-week notice period. I am ready to apply my expertise to help Council share project milestones and engage community.

highlights: Led creative direction and produced 50+ video assets for social media platforms achieving 30% higher engagement
Managed multi-channel content distribution strategies (Instagram, Facebook, TikTok) reaching 200K+ targeted users
Directed and edited video content optimized for short-form social engagement, reducing production time by 20%

experience.role: Graphic Designer + Comms and Client Management
highlights: Led rebranding initiatives and social media crisis communications strategies for 5+ clients
Developed 100+ WCAG-compliant digital assets for government social channels ensuring accessibility
Managed social media engagement and brand consistency across diverse client portfolios, increasing reach by 40%
Created 80+ digital collateral and social media assets adhering to strict government brand guidelines
Supported high-level digital reporting and campaign analytics tracking, improving ROI by 15%
Delivered advanced content on photography, video production, and social media industry trends to 40+ students
Mentored 20+ students on digital marketing strategies and portfolio development
Directed creative social campaigns and cross-platform digital operations for 20+ clients
Produced 200+ video and graphic assets for social media, websites, and external media
Led classes in UX/UI Design, Audiovisual Media, and Digital Communication to 100+ students
Developed curriculum integrating Adobe Creative Suite and social media strategy

education.degree: Bachelor of Audiovisual Communication & Digital Design

jobDescription:
Role Overview
We are seeking a talented Web Designer & Social Media Creator to manage and enhance our digital platforms. This role combines creative design, website management, and social media content creation to ensure a consistent and engaging brand experience.

Key Responsibilities
Design, update, and maintain the Moto National websites

Create visually appealing graphics and digital assets

Develop and schedule engaging social media content across platforms

Manage social media accounts (e.g., Instagram, Facebook, TikTok)

Monitor performance metrics and optimize campaigns

Ensure brand consistency across all digital channels

Collaborate with marketing team on campaigns and promotions

Requirements
Experience in web design (HTML, CSS, or platforms like Shopify/WordPress)

Strong graphic design skills (Adobe Creative Suite)

Proven experience managing social media accounts

Understanding of social media trends and digital marketing strategies

Strong attention to detail and creativity

Ability to work independently and meet deadlines

Nice to Have
Experience in the automotive or motorcycle industry

Basic video editing skills

Knowledge of SEO and analytics tools



Employer questions
Your application will include the following questions:
Which of the following statements best describes your right to work in Australia?
How many years' experience do you have as a Social Media Content Creator?
Which of the following Adobe products are you experienced with?
How many years' experience do you have in social media marketing?
Do you have experience with Search Engine Optimisation (SEO)?
Are you experienced with copy writing and content creation?
Have you completed a qualification in marketing?
How much notice are you required to give your current employer?

data.persona: Government-grade creative leader with 23 years of full-spectrum production experience, now applying digital strategy and social media expertise to drive Moto National's brand consistency and engagement.
data.professionalProfile: With 23 years of senior creative leadership across government, agency, and entrepreneurial settings, I bring full-spectrum production—strategy, design, video, and social media—to Moto National. I have deep experience in web design (HTML, CSS, WordPress, Shopify), graphic design (Adobe Creative Suite: Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, Premiere Pro, After Effects), social media management, SEO, and copywriting. I am an Australian Permanent Resident, available with a 4-week notice period, and hold a Bachelor of Audiovisual Communication & Digital Design. I am also a former Lecturer and ClickUp expert, skilled in building creative capability and systems.
data.coverLetter:
I am an Australian Permanent Resident with 23 years of senior creative leadership, ready to solve Moto National's digital content and brand consistency challenges. I have 6+ years of experience as a Social Media Content Creator and 6+ years in social media marketing, managing Instagram, Facebook, and TikTok for brands like Crewcible and Dionysus. I am experienced with Adobe Creative Suite—specifically Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, Premiere Pro, and After Effects—and I have extensive experience in Search Engine Optimisation (SEO) and copywriting. I also hold a Bachelor of Audiovisual Communication & Digital Design. My notice period is 4 weeks.

I design and maintain websites using WordPress and Shopify, HTML/CSS, and ensure WCAG compliance for government-grade accessibility. I create graphics, edit videos, and develop social media content that drives engagement and brand consistency. At Dionysus, I led rebranding and crisis communications across digital channels. At Crewcible, I directed social media content strategies and optimized for short-form video. I also mentor teams and implement systems like ClickUp to streamline workflows.

I am confident I can elevate Moto National's digital presence from strategy to execution.
